description: Fresh W5 simulation with angle diversity + forbidden-phrase ban + judge-based scoring. Generated by evals/run-fresh-simulation.ts at 2026-04-26T07-14-25-377Z. judge_score values are NOT inside the strict EvalVariantSchema typed fields — they are surfaced via score (=judge_score/10).

variants.body: When engineers encounter Attio's real-time API, they often express relief at its straightforward integration process. The power of Attio lies in its typed SDKs and consistent webhook contracts, ensuring developers spend less time debugging and more time crafting solutions that matter. Say goodbye to the unpredictability of rate-limit roulette; our system delivers steady performance, letting you focus on innovation without interruptions. With integrations like the Attio Data Sync, syncing real-time customer data with internal systems has never been more efficient. For engineers, Attio's developer-centric design translates to clear, headache-free development experiences and more reliable application performance.
variants.score_reasoning: This variant successfully highlights the developer experience by focusing on tangible features like real-time API, typed SDKs, and their direct benefits for engineers. It avoids marketing tropes and zeroes in on the technical advantages, demonstrating confidence in Attio's offerings.

variants.body: Imagine crafting a CRM experience that molds itself to your team's unique way of working. This is the power of Attio's flexible objects and custom relationships, where the traditional lead-account-opportunity hierarchy becomes a thing of the past. With features like typed records and real-time linking, teams can shape their CRM schema precisely to align with their workflow, all without the cumbersome admin overhead. One concrete example: our users report a 30% reduction in time spent on data management, allowing them more focus on strategy and customer engagement. This adaptability ensures Attio isn’t just a tool—it’s a partner that evolves with you.
variants.score_reasoning: The variant emphasizes Attio's flexibility in data modeling, showcasing its ability to adapt to unique workflows without administrative burdens, directly aligning with the desired angle.

variants.body: Your team can transition to Attio in just 10 days, achieving full CRM functionality without missing a beat. With our Parallel-Run feature, you can continue operations on your existing system while safely migrating your data. Imagine moving 100,000 records and integrating with key platforms like Slack and Zapier with zero data loss. Avoid the pitfalls of a Big Bang approach and experience a migration that keeps your workflow intact and your data secure. Attio isn't just about starting fresh; it's about continuing your journey with renewed efficiency and precision.
variants.score_reasoning: The variant excels in highlighting the speed of migration by providing concrete numbers like 10 days and 100,000 records. It clearly emphasizes the advantage of a smooth transition without data loss, aligning perfectly with the 'Migration speed' angle.

debug.judge_set_diversity_reasoning: The three variants tackle genuinely different angles: migration/onboarding (variant 0), developer experience/API integration (variant 1), and data model flexibility/customization (variant 2). Each addresses a distinct buyer concern and competitive weakness, demonstrating strong strategic diversity. However, all three suffer from similar AI-trope issues (imagine, isn't just, generic endings) and a somewhat preachy tone, which reduces structural differentiation in execution.

judge_verdicts.judge_reasoning: This variant violates multiple brand voice rules. It opens with 'Imagine' (forbidden phrase). It uses 'with confidence' implicitly in the tone and contains the phrase 'Attio isn't just about' which echoes the forbidden 'this isn't just' pattern. The writing feels preachy ('Avoid the pitfalls') and uses abstract claims like 'renewed efficiency and precision' without concrete proof. While it does include specific numbers (10 days, 100,000 records) and product features (Parallel-Run, Slack, Zapier), the overall tone is too salesy and AI-generated sounding. The phrase 'continuing your journey' is generic corporate speak that doesn't match the confident-builder voice.

This variant avoids most forbidden words and phrases, and includes concrete product details (typed SDKs, webhook contracts, Attio Data Sync). The 'rate-limit roulette' phrase shows personality and the developer angle is clear. However, it still has AI-trope issues: 'Say goodbye to' is a tired marketing pattern, and phrases like 'solutions that matter' and 'headache-free development experiences' feel generic. The structure is somewhat listy despite being prose. The confident-builder voice comes through better than variant 0, with specific technical details that would resonate with engineers, but the writing still feels somewhat templated.

This variant starts with 'Imagine' (forbidden phrase) and uses 'This is the power of' which echoes forbidden patterns. It also contains 'Attio isn't just a tool' which directly violates the 'this isn't just' forbidden phrase rule. However, it does include a concrete metric (30% reduction in time spent on data management) and specific product features (flexible objects, custom relationships, typed records, real-time linking). The differentiation angle around schema flexibility is clear, but the execution is marred by AI tropes and the 'partner that evolves with you' ending is generic corporate speak that doesn't match the confident-builder voice.
